to retrieve all versions of a particular investment guideline.
            Note: This is not the ID you use for the other API requests, but a shared ID across versions (the `ClientInvestmentGuidelineId` field).

parameters.name: includeInactive
parameters.description: to retrieve active and inactive versions. By default only active versions are sent.

get.description:
Retrieve a specific investment guideline. Note that an investment guideline can have multiple versions.
Each version has a different 'id' but shares the same 'clientInvestmentGuidelineId'.
Additionally there is a notable difference between 'node-type' and 'owner' rows for the metric- and exposure-matrix:
An empty node-type row means the row falls-back to the next higher level (custodian -> legalEntity -> legalEntityGroup -> client).
An empty owner row (for example for a particular legal entity) means no exposures are enabled for this particular owner and matrix.
So the API is similar to the behavior of the QPLIX-UI.

parameters.name: clientId
parameters.description: the client id of the guideline

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: the id (the actual 'id' field, not the 'clientInvestmentGuidelineId' field) of the investment guideline

Qplix.UI.Web.Api.Public.v1.DataContracts.description:
Data contract for classification export matching the SQL schema.
Maps MongoDB SecurityClassification to the classification table structure.

Data contract for entity export matching the SQL schema.
Maps MongoDB LegalEntity/VirtualEntity to the entity table structure.

Data contract for investment export matching the SQL schema.
Maps MongoDB Ownership to the investment table structure.

Data contract for point-in-time data export matching the SQL schema.
Maps single-value metrics to the point_in_time table structure.

Data contract for point-in-time dimensional data export matching the SQL schema.
Maps dimensional metrics to the point_in_time_dimensional table structure.

Data contract for security export matching the SQL schema.
Maps MongoDB Security to the security table structure.

Data contract for time series metadata export matching the SQL schema.
Maps the time series definition from query results to the time_series table structure.

Data contract for time series data point export matching the SQL schema.
Maps individual time series data points to the time_series_data_point table structure.
